notes.purpose: Open visual aids and simple diagrams for lesson pages.
notes.attribution: Images are remote educational assets with source links. Diagrams are original app teaching aids.

Place Value.title: Place value chart
Place Value.caption: Shows how each digit changes value by its column.

Angles.title: Angle diagram
Angles.caption: Use the arms and turn to identify acute, right, obtuse, straight, and reflex angles.

Subtraction / Addition.title: Subtraction model
Subtraction / Addition.caption: Visual subtraction helps students see taking away and checking columns.

Multiplying.title: Multiplication groups
Multiplying.caption: Multiplication is equal groups before it becomes column working.

Area / Perimeter.title: Area model
Area / Perimeter.caption: Students compare space inside a shape with distance around the shape.

Number Pattern.title: Number pattern ladder
Number Pattern.caption: Look for the change from one number to the next, then apply the same rule.
points: Look for the change from one number to the next

Novel.title: Story mountain
Novel.caption: A story mountain helps students see beginning, build-up, problem, climax, and resolution.
points: A story mountain helps students see beginning

Media Explosion.title: Media message map
Media Explosion.caption: Students inspect sender, audience, message, purpose, and action.

Grammar (Noun, Verb).title: Sentence parts chart
Grammar (Noun, Verb).caption: A sentence chart separates naming words, action words, describing words, and extra details.
points: A sentence chart separates naming words

Vocabulary.title: Context clue web
Vocabulary.caption: A clue web reminds students to use nearby words, examples, and results.
points: A clue web reminds students to use nearby words

Reading Comprehension.title: Evidence ladder
Reading Comprehension.caption: Students climb from literal facts to inference using evidence.
points: Students climb from literal facts to inference using evidence.

Punctuation.title: Punctuation toolbox
Punctuation.caption: Students choose full stops, commas, question marks, exclamation marks, and speech marks.

Geographers Tools.title: Compass
Geographers Tools.caption: A compass image helps students connect directions with real field tools.

Representing Geography.title: Map example
Representing Geography.caption: Maps use symbols, scale, direction, and keys to represent real places.

Creating Maps of Vanuatu.title: Vanuatu
Creating Maps of Vanuatu.caption: Use with a province map activity to locate islands, towns, and provinces.

The New Hebrides Condominium.title: New Hebrides flag
The New Hebrides Condominium.caption: A colonial-era symbol helps students separate New Hebrides history from modern Vanuatu.

Road to Independence.title: Timeline
Road to Independence.caption: A timeline places New Hebrides, independence, symbols, and national identity in order.

Origin of Culture / Protecting our Herit.title: Cultural heritage
Origin of Culture / Protecting our Herit.caption: Use the image to discuss objects, stories, places, dances, and customs that people protect.

Preserve the Environment.title: Nature conservation
Preserve the Environment.caption: Students can compare damaged land with healthy habitats and tabu areas.

Vanuatu National Symbols, Geography & Tr.title: Flag of Vanuatu
Vanuatu National Symbols, Geography & Tr.caption: The Vanuatu flag: Red (blood unity), Black (Melanesian people), Green (rich land), Yellow (Christianity light). The boar's tusk and namele leaves symbolize prosperity and peace.

Vanuatu National Symbols, Geography & Tr.title: Flag Color Meanings
Vanuatu National Symbols, Geography & Tr.caption: Remember what each color on the Vanuatu flag represents.
points: RED = The blood that unites all ni-Vanuatu people
BLACK = The Melanesian people and our heritage
GREEN = The richness of our islands and agriculture
YELLOW = The light of Christianity and peace
Namele Leaves = Peace and custom law

Vanuatu National Symbols, Geography & Tr.title: Vanuatu Geography Facts
Vanuatu National Symbols, Geography & Tr.caption: Key geographical features of Vanuatu to remember for your exam.
points: Located on the Pacific Ring of Fire
Has many active volcanoes (Mt. Yasur on Tanna, Mt. Garet on Gaua)
Frequent earthquakes due to moving tectonic plates
Six provinces from north to south: Torba, Sanma, Penama, Malampa, Shefa, Tafea

Vanuatu National Symbols, Geography & Tr.title: National Symbols Summary
Vanuatu National Symbols, Geography & Tr.caption: Key national symbols every Grade 6 student should know.
points: Anthem: 'Yumi, Yumi, Yumi' by François Vincent Ayssav
Motto: 'Long God yumi stanap' by Iawawoi Rezel Samana
Nagol: Pentecost Island land diving — men only (test of bravery)
Aneityum: Chiefs buried under house floor, head exposed for veneration

Food and Drinking Packaging.title: Food packaging
Food and Drinking Packaging.caption: Packaging visuals help students inspect labels, dates, ingredients, and safety signs.

Fresh Food.title: Healthy food display
Fresh Food.caption: Students can group foods by energy, growth, protection, and everyday balance.

Making Choices - What and Where.title: Healthy diet
Making Choices - What and Where.caption: Use the picture to compare healthy choices with processed food choices.

Special Food Needs.title: Special diet plate
Special Food Needs.caption: Students compare food needs for babies, pregnant mothers, sick people, elderly people, and active children.
points: Students compare food needs for babies

Keep it Balanced.title: Balanced food choices
Keep it Balanced.caption: A balanced diet has many kinds of food, enough water, and sensible portions.

Clean Drinking Water.title: Clean drinking water
Clean Drinking Water.caption: Connect the picture to safe collection, boiling, covering, and storage.

Immunisation.title: Vaccine
Immunisation.caption: Students can see vaccines as prevention that trains the body before illness arrives.

Malaria.title: Malaria parasite
Malaria.caption: Use with mosquito life-cycle and net-use discussion.

Diabetes.title: Diabetes symbol
Diabetes.caption: A simple symbol anchors discussion of blood sugar, insulin, food, and exercise.

Disease.title: Disease concept
Disease.caption: Use carefully to discuss symptoms, causes, spread, prevention, and care.

What is Compost.title: Compost pile
What is Compost.caption: Students can identify brown materials, green materials, air, water, and decomposers.

How Composting Process Works.title: Compost process
How Composting Process Works.caption: Shows compost as a process from organic waste to rich soil.

Pest.title: Pest organism
Pest.caption: Students can distinguish harmful pests from helpful organisms.

Friendly Organisms.title: Earthworm
Friendly Organisms.caption: Earthworms are a clear example of organisms that improve soil.

Living Things and the Environment.title: Habitat web
Living Things and the Environment.caption: A habitat web links living things with sunlight, soil, water, air, and shelter.
points: A habitat web links living things with sunlight

Food Chain and Food Web.title: Food chain arrows
Food Chain and Food Web.caption: Arrows show energy moving from producer to consumer to predator.
points: Arrows show energy moving from producer to consumer to predator.

Photosynthesis.title: Photosynthesis diagram
Photosynthesis.caption: The diagram connects sunlight, water, carbon dioxide, glucose, and oxygen.

Flowering Plants.title: Flowering plant
Flowering Plants.caption: A real flower helps students identify petals, stamen, pistil, stigma, and pollen.

Respiratory and Circulatory System.title: Body systems loop
Respiratory and Circulatory System.caption: The lungs bring oxygen in; the heart and blood move it around the body.
points: the heart and blood move it around the body.
